ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Compel Account Bifurcated As To Remaining Issues Of Objections To Purported Account; Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-18-2016 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Compel Account Bifurcated As To Remaining Issues Of Objections To Purported Account; Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Continued To Oct-18-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Chris Elzi for and with petitioners Robert Abigana and John Abigana; Jim Bridgman for Carol Burns.

Mr. Bridgman stated that he had submitted an accounting, and that no objections to it had been filed. Mr. Elzi responded that Mr. Bridgman was well aware of the petitioners' objections to the accounting. He apologized to the Court for his failure to file formal objections within the time set by the Court. The Court stated that the parties had three alternatives: either resolve their differences, Mr. Elzi file an amended petition, or Mr. Bridgman file a petition for approval of the accounting. He encouraged the parties to consider participation in pro bono mediation. Both counsel agreed to participate in the Court's pro bono mediation program. The Court will issue an order for mediation to be completed by October 7, 2016, and directed the filing of a status report by October 12, 2016. The matter was continued to October 18, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Compel Account Bifurcated As To Remaining Issues Of Objections To Purported Account; Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-19-2016 Continued To Aug-02-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Compel Account Bifurcated As To Remaining Issues Of Objections To Purported Account; Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-07-2016 Continued To Jul-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgeman, for and with Carol Burns; Chris Elzi (by CourtCall) for petitioner Robert Abigana.

Mr. Bridgeman reported thatthe accounting has now been filed, and that a plan of distribution is in place. Mr. Elzi responded that he has reviewed the accounting and found it unacceptable. He stated that he would like to discuss the matter further with Mr. Bridgeman in an attempt to work things out. He added that if the parties are unable to reach agreement, he will need to file an amended petition, and would need three weeks to file it. The matter was continued to July 19, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy John Boncato. (Not reported)

Petition To Compel Account; Objections To Purported Account; For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; And Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar May-31-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgman for and with (trustee) Carol Burns. Chris Elzi (via court call) for (petitioners) Robert & John Abigana.

The Court asked where the accounting was and reminded counsel that at the last hearing it was promised in two weeks. Mr. Bridgman stated that they had an issue getting the stock dividends and that they now have the information. Mr. Elzi stated that this has been continued since 12/8/15, that on 2/19/16 he received an email stating that it would be pointless to go forward with the hearing, that on 3/25/16 he was told that his questions would be answered, that there have been 3 hearings which are expensive, that they cannot rely on Mr. Bridgman's word, that Ms. Burns has been the trustee since 2008, that there is no reason to believe that this will get done, that they are asking for sanctions but are not sure what the court can impose, that the ultimate sanction would be to remove Ms. Burns and asks the court to ensure that they will get something. The Court stated that it will grant the portion of the petition compelling the accounting, that the balance of the petition will be bifurcated and continued to June 7, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court further stated that the accounting is now ordered, that it is to be completed and filed no later than June 2, 2016, that Mr. Elzi is to prepare a form of order reflecting the bifurcation and that on June 7, 2016 if the parties have not stipulated to a continuance they will discuss how to proceed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Account; Objections To Purported Account; For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; And Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar May-03-2016 Continued To May-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgman for (trustee) Carol Burns. Chris Elzi (via court call) for (petitioners) Robert & John Abigana.

The Court stated that the petitions are still out there and asked if there has been resolution. Mr. Bridgman stated that there has not been resolution, that he has been unable to get the accounting completed and asked for an additional 2 weeks to get it completed. The Court stated that delivery of the accounting may give everyone what they need. Mr. Elzi stated that he has no objections to the continuance but has concerns that even with the accounting they will not know what they need to know. The Court continued the petition to compel an accounting, etc., to May 24, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 instructing Mr. Bridgman to get it completed so that Mr. Elzi's clients can review it. Mr. Elzi requested that he receive the accounting at least a week prior to the hearing date so that it can be reviewed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Account; Objections To Purported Account; For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; And Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-05-2016 Continued To May-03-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Chris Elzi (via court call) for (petitioner) Robert Abigana. James Bridgman for and with (trustee) Carol Burns.

The Court asked for a status on resolution. Mr. Bridgman reported that the property was sold on 2/11/16, that there was a tentative agreement that he would file the accounting within 45 days, that the accounting is in progress and asked for a 30 day continuance. Mr. Elzi stated that his predecessor asked for an accounting on 2/17/15, that this is a simple trust, that they do not know why an additional 30 days is needed, that they request that the accounting be done by 4/19/16, that the trustee offered to distribute $25,000.00 to each beneficiary and that there needs to be an order to that effect, that the longer this goes on the less his clients trust the trustee, that they would like confirmation of the amount in the bank account, that they need an additional preliminary distribution and instead of $25,000.00 they want $35,000.00. Mr. Bridgman stated that there has already been one preliminary distribution of $50,000.00, that they are not sure what the taxes and capital gains will be regarding the sale of property, confirmed that the account has about $128,000.00 in it currently and that the accountant is working on the taxes now. The court asked if the accounting can be done prior to 5/1/16. Mr. Bridgman responded that it could not, that they had previous accountants in the past, that he has personal issues that he is dealing with and that further distribution is not appropriate now. Mr. Elzi stated that they had offered $25,000.00 to each beneficiary, that he thinks it is appropriate and that the beneficiaries will be issued a K-1 and will be liable for their own taxes. The Court asked if Mr. Bridgman had offered the $25,000.00 distribution in addition to the $50,000.00 already distributed. Mr. Elzi stated yes, that they do not think there is a need to postpone the distribution, that his clients need assurances that the money is in the bank and confirmed that May 1, 2016 would be fine. Mr. Elzi further stated that this is a simple trust with one major asset that has been sold. The Court asked Mr. Bridgman if he could provide a declaration indicating the amount of money that is in the bank account. Mr. Bridgman stated that it is extra work, that these hearings are expensive, that the beneficiaries keep requesting more "make" work and that their attitude towards the trustee is insulting. The Court stated that Mr. Bridgman needs to satisfy Mr. Elzi as to what is in the bank. The petition to compel account; objections to purported account, etc., was continued to May 3, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Account; Objections To Purported Account; For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; And Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-23-2016 Continued To Apr-05-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Chris Elzi (via court call) for (petitioner) Robert Abigana. James Bridgman for and with (trustee) Carol Burns.

Mr. Elzi reported that many of the issues are being slowly resolved, that the home has been sold, that they have asked for an accounting and believe Mr. Bridgman will submit one, that they have received a progress report, that they had agreed to a continuance of today's hearing but requested it too late, that a final accounting is to be submitted within 45 days, that Robert has tried to go through the information and has come up with $35,000.00 in legal fees and distribution. Mr. Bridgman confirmed that they have a general agreement that that they will produce a formal accounting in 45 days. The Court stated that absent the parties reaching an agreement they can try to mediate otherwise the matter is under court supervision. Mr. Bridgman stated that the current amount in the trust is $280,000.00, that they would like to distribute $50,000.00 to each beneficiary leaving a reserve in case issues arise later and will do a full and final accounting and distribution. The Court stated that it is willing to continue for 45 days for the petition to be filed, that the reason this trust is before the court is because of the current petition and that if everyone agrees on how to distribute it can go off calendar. Mr. Bridgman stated that if Mr. Elzi accepts the accounting he can dismiss his petition. Mr. Elzi confirmed that the initial distribution will be $50,000.00. The petition to compel and account, etc., was continued to April 5, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that a petition for final account will be filed prior to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
